courseleaf.title: Education, Master of Arts
courseleaf.informationtext:
Admissions are suspended while our faculty review the program’s current topical focus and requirements. We are not offering the Master of Arts General Track for this admissions cycle.

Graduate Study

The Master of Arts in Education provides a course of study for examining research and theory about learners, teachers, schools, and related social institutions. The program prepares professionals to conduct research about the education of children, youth, and adults in a multicultural society. Graduates may assume leadership positions in school districts, state education agencies, and private organizations concerned with instructional research, policy and practice.

Degree Requirements & Program Coordinator

See Education (M.A.).

Courses

See Education (EDU).
courseleaf.quickviewtext: The one-year MA in Education focuses on Educational Assessment and Measurement. Students have the unique opportunity to build their capacity to develop and use educational assessments, deepen their substantive knowledge of issues in educational measurement, and apply and interpret quantitative research methods to address educational problems.
